entry.title: Re: BindingObject of a selected list picker item
entry.content: First thing would be to verify the MDK version in use on the tablet.  Access to the list picker binding object is only available in MDK 24.4 and above.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content: Hi @Amit56 - This must be very frustrating for you. Normally, once you get Composable Storefront installed, it just works. From the error message you are getting, it sounds like it's a timing issue. Have you considered trying to access the site using another browser? If you're using Chrome, try Edge or Opera, and see if you're getting the same errors. If that doesn't work, I can't think of anything other than opening a service ticket. Regretfully, I don't have the necessary Angular expertise to provide more useful insight.

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE backup server
entry.content: Thank you Chris

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: It is a light. It illuminates the desk without reflecting on the screen.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: My workplace 🙂

entry.title: Re: BindingObject of a selected list picker item
entry.content:
access the selected value from the ListPicker, such as using a TargetPath to retrieve the binding directly:

 

 

let selectedBinding = clientAPI.evaluateTargetPath("Page:CreateSalesOrderItem/Control:ListPicker/SelectedValue");

entry.title: SAP GUI on ARM Windows clients
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

As the ARM Architecture is now also becoming more and more relevant also on WINDOWS client the company is starting to invest in such clients.

We would like now to prepare for the immediate future and avoid issues and problems as good as possible.

We are aware of SNOTE . Also do you have experience running SAP GUI for Windows on ARM with an emulator? If yes it it working without issue and we can just use this?

The SNOTE also states regarding to ARM "If you want to use such a platform, we recommend that you check whether you can use a different SAP GUI version than SAP GUI for Windows. " 
What other SAP GUI versions are meant? I checked and there is no SAP GUI for JAVA ARM version available. And SAP GUI for HTML simply does not provide the same functionality.

 

Thanks and best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI on ARM Windows clients
entry.content:
Hello, 

The SAP GUI for Java ARM64 bit version is available for MacOS only and it can be downloaded in the below link:

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenterviewer/73555000100200015948/MAINT

The only SAP GUI on ARM64 is available for MacOS.

As per SAP Note 66971 , there isn't any plan to publish a version for SAP GUI for Windows.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: My friend "Samy"

entry.title: Re: Outbound delivery from ERP is not replicating to EWM
entry.content:
You have the answer in front of you. You have not configured the item type as described in the message. 

if you open the log for the message you usually get a IMG path for the missing config. 
/Jacob

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone - 404 error - App - NOT FOUND
entry.content: Can someone provide a more detailed resolution because I am not sure where to find the system aliases used.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP and Third-Party Cookies Deprecation
entry.content:
Hello Markus,

I have a similar question as Vinod. 

The suggested solutions in the documentations are either one of Partitioned cookies, Storage Access API or OAuth PKCE flow. 

However, in the BTP context is it sufficient to use Trusted domains to work as frame ancestor, eliminating the need for taking care of third party cookies deprecation matter? 

more specifically, are the followings enough in the scenario that Build WZ is used for the managed app router and used with SAP IAS, then want to embed the Fiori apps deployed in HTML5 repository to ERP, in my case SAP Business One web client? 

1. for the BTP subaccount hosting WZ, maintain a trusted domain, e.g., https://erp-acme.com in Security > Settings > Trusted Domains, if WZ doesn't use the same custom domain as ERP.

2. for the IAS tenant, maintain a trusted domain, e.g., erp-acme.com in Applications & Resources > Tenant Settings > Customization > Trusted Domains.

 

Regards
EunSeok

entry.title: Re: Getting issue in TDS Calcualtion, Base on LDC Certifi...
entry.content: Do not maintain exemption details in tab Vendor: Withholding Tax for TAN exemption

entry.title: Typing a value doesn't show any value from dropdown in Advanced Search BackOffice
entry.content:
In BackOffice advanced search, when I type a value, it doesn't show any result. It's showing blank. How can I fix it?

This is from the dropdown:

When I typed SAMPLE_NUMBER_1, it doesn't show the SAMPLE_NUMBER_1 object to select.

Attribute B is TypeB.

I have this

<context type="TypeA" component="advanced-search" merge-by="type">
   <as:advanced-search xmlns:as="http://www.hybris.com/cockpitng/config/advancedsearch" connection-operator="AND">
      <as:field-list>
         ...
         <as:field name="attributeB" operator="equals" selected="true"/>
      </as:field-list>
   </as:advanced-search>
</context>

entry.title: Buttons for accept/reject do not work - CRM IC
entry.content:
Dear consultant,

we are facing an issue in our production system with the Accept and Reject buttons.

Sometimes the buttons do not work. There are blinking and waiting for click by user, the user tryes to click, but the buttons are still blinking. This behaviour is random, ot is not possible to simulate it. This happens  2-3times a day by more users.

We are facing this problem 2-3weeks.

We implement the following notes, but without progress...

0002821466
0003380982
0003383396
0003445773
0003501258

Thank you for your help. For better idea see the attachement.

Best regards

Milan Materna

entry.title: Re: Challenges in Cross Border Data Flows and Data Localization amidst new Regulations
entry.content:
Here is how one client addressed.... Does this also address the requirement for the creation of the Candidate Profile? @keishaeverett 

  • The ask of making fields reportable is a configuration task customers have access to do. 
  • We have figured out the way to proceed with our legal counsel.
  • We will be using the new fields in Candidate Profile to capture these consents.

  •  

  • I appreciate if you can make these 2 fields reportable in Acceptance Env (------T2), so that our business can start the testing.
  • id="custCN3rdPartyConsent"
  • id="custCNOverseasConsent"

entry.title: Re: Challenges in Cross Border Data Flows and Data Localization amidst new Regulations
entry.content: FYI, "reportable fields" changes must be done by the customer / partner.

entry.title: Hello Everyone
entry.content:
My name is Lizanne Ramutsindela  and I'm excited to participate in the Digital Skills Initiative because I believe that enhancing my digital competencies is crucial for staying relevant in today’s fast-paced work environment. With technology continuously reshaping how we work, I’m eager to expand my skill set and leverage digital tools to drive better outcomes in my role.

A bit about myself: I have three years of experience in Human Resources, where I've developed a strong foundation in talent acquisition, employee engagement, and organizational development. I enjoy collaborating with diverse teams and creating inclusive environments that foster growth and innovation. My experience has shown me the importance of data-driven decision-making, and I recognize that enhancing my digital skills will enable me to analyze HR metrics more effectively and implement strategic initiatives that align with business goals.

I believe I'm a good fit for the program because of my passion for learning and adapting to new technologies. I’m committed to continuous professional development and keen on using digital tools to enhance HR practices. By participating in this initiative, I hope to not only improve my own skills but also bring valuable insights back to my organization, ultimately contributing to a more agile and forward-thinking HR function.

I'm looking forward to learning and growing with you all.

Kind Regards

Lizanne Ramutsindela

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Public Cloud Best Practices PP Module
entry.content:
Hello

Here is the link for the S/4HANA Cloud Best Practices in the Signavio Process Navigator:

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408?region=DE

If you choose Manufacturing and then Production Planning, you will get the related best practices. 

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: My laptop setup

entry.title: USMM2 measurment 1160 fails with error You are not authorized to execute this program.
entry.content:
In my monthly run of USMM2 in a SAP ERP 7.50 system, the Measurement of application 1160 ("SAP Solutions by OpenText") fails with an error "You are not authorized to execute this program." (Message no. /OTX/PF09_M_001023). Apparently this is a new measurement, because it is not present in last month's report. 
In september we updated component OTEXVIM to version 80700_006, SP 0005.
Did anyone else encounter the same error and have any idea how to solve the authorization issue?

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Hi,

Same issue for me as well. Badge for this task #111D2A is missing

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
This is my workspace. It may look a bit cluttered, but this is where all the ABAP magic happens—though the real clutter is inside this setup!

My laptop is the heart of my operations, running an instance of ABAP AS 7.52 SP04 almost all the time. I frequently use it to test solution designs for my work, especially when I need to experiment with different approaches. Recently, I’ve been diving into the basics of SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming), using Docker containers alongside Visual Studio Code.

For my main work setup, things get a bit more complex. I connect via WireGuard to a mini-server (the small black box under my Bowser and Marty McFly figures). On this server, I run a Docker container with the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022, as well as a VMware VM hosting IDES EhP8. My primary work machine operates from here as a VirtualBox VM—this setup gives me the isolation and flexibility I need.

In my off-hours, I enjoy experimenting with integrating services from these demo servers using SAP Cloud Connector.

The second photo shows my Stream Deck, loaded with all my current shortcuts. It’s been a game-changer, especially for learning and quickly accessing the most common shortcuts in ADT Eclipse.

I’ve been working from home since 2019, and that explains the rest of my setup—microphone, and all the other gear I need to stay connected and productive!

entry.title: Re: Simplify SSO with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) & SAP Identity Authentication Service
entry.content:
Thanks very nice blog @PolySonika. .

@NitishKommara You can use sign on URL

https://{tenant}.trial-accounts.ondemand.com for this if you are using trial account.
 
I found this from one of the youtube link. This can be common across all the SAP solutions.
Ref:
https://youtu.be/xnhUfSvMXHI

entry.title: Re: Checks to Troubleshoot Issues Related to Initiate Onboarding from Recruiting
entry.content:
Nice Blog Thanks for sharing. I just came across today in search of one issue which I am currently facing right now. If you know the solution.

Issue:-One of candidate onboarding initiated as the hire date would be 10/01/2024 and then that is cancelled, and his onboarding is restarted as hire date would be 9/24/2024, as HR decided to hire him little early as he completes his documentation. Now the candidate is appearing on MPH, and we are trying to complete the hiring ( Newhire Date-09/24/2024), but we are getting an error under compensation portlet as " Employee XXXX does not have Valid Job Information data on 09/24/2024. 

Then we assumed let's hire him with 10/01/2024 and then we can correct his hire date via "hire date correction tool" still showing an application error.

We cannot directly hire from EC as the duplicate SSN error is there.

What would be the best solution, and what I am missing here to fix this. 

Note: - I have already run the validation under check tool, didn't find anything.

Thanks in advance.

RasmitaParida

entry.title: SAP HANA Unique Constraint Violated Error [301]
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In our SAP HANA database, we're adding a row to a table with a query from MII Workbench when our machine creates a product. When we trying to add, query returns this message;

 

com.sap.xmii.Illuminator.logging.LHException: com.sap.sql.exception.OpenSQLIntegrityConstraintViolationException: [301]: unique constraint violated: Table(CURING_LOG), Index(_SYS_TREE_CS_#199412_#0_#P0) with error: unique constraint violation on pos=0 for table OMQ::CURING:CURING_LOG$delta_1$en, key: $trexexternalkey$=2024-10-04;23:17:04;5,DNZ5R already exists as udiv=24121, indexname=_SYS_TREE_CS_#199412_#0_#P0

               OpenSQLExceptionCategories: [NON_TRANSIENT, INTEGRITY_CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION]

Our table's constraint are 3 columns ; ZBITIS_T , ZBITIS_S and ARBPL as shown in below ;

and the _SYS_TREE_CS_#199412_#0_#P0 is this ;

What I understand, error message tells me that I'm trying to add a record that already exist in the table. There has to be a record that equals in ZBITIS_T, ZBITIS_S and ARBPL with the record that I'm trying to. But there is not. I'm certain that we trying to write record only once. 

And somehow, record writes to table successfully but that error message comes also.

So my question is to you, how could this happening ? Why am I seeing that error message even the record can be seen in table ?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: DevSecOps for SAP S/4HANA Rise
entry.content: Late last year 2023, US Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued list of 42 observed frequently exploited vulnerabilities –under Advisory Alert Code AA23-215A. ERP software vulnerabilities (for Oracle and SAP) made to this first time in 2022. This stresses the need to “shift left” and ensure new business-critical applications are provisioned securely from day one. Implementing a security-by-design approach, following the NIST Cybersecurity Framework, automated patch management process and Point-in-time vulnerability assessment for preemptively detecting vulnerabilities and preventing cyberattacks targeting critical data and systems is the need of the hour. Securing the custom code and seeking and using targeted threat intelligence to stay ahead of zero-day threats is paramount to the security of SAP Systems

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for Credit Memo Request Workflow
entry.content:
I will also like to know why email notification are not sent to the requestor each time the credit memo request for approval has been released, rejected and reworked.

I have copied the predelivered CMR templates as described above.

entry.title: Re: Flexibility Services LrepConnector doesn't seem to work
entry.content: so that it returns the flexibility changes files and those changes can be reflected in the front end

entry.title: Re: How to implement flagging function in sac analytical...
entry.content:
First, create a live data connection to BW or a model with write-back enabled. Create a planning table with necessary dimensions and the status column. status column should be editable.

Implement input controls in your SAC application that allow users to select the desired row and update the status.

Create data action to write-back to BW system. This will be triggered when a user updates the status. Link the data action to the relevant event in your planning application like a button click or a row update.

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management errors in taxonomy mapping...
entry.content:
This issue is being reviewed by SAP Support and appears to be a bug

Marc Kuipers

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC SAP TM Archiving TOR Object
entry.content: @Rohit_Mahajan   Thanks for your blog and we are planning to start this sooner.  I think archival also has a per-requisite for a freight order to be in completed status.  For old documents that are not yet completed can be done using standard report program:  /SCMTMS/TOR_SET_COMPLETED  We can also set the residence period here.

Vishala.

entry.title: Re: Posting not possible; new Asset Accounting is inconsi...

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
SAP Concur - Managing Invoice Vendors via API
entry.content:
I initially began my career at SAP Concur as an Implementation Project Manager, where my responsibilities included project management and functional design work. As I transitioned into my current role as a Technical Consultant, my focus has shifted towards data integration. In this blog series, I will guide you through the Invoice APIs available via Client Web Services, enabling you to streamline your entire Requisition-to-Payment process. In this first installment, we'll dive into the task of creating and managing vendor records, which serve as the foundation of the entire Invoice module. 

 

Prerequisites

You must be contracted for Invoice Processing and Client Web Services.

This post will not discuss authentication. Please see this post for an introduction to authentication and web service application building within Concur. Please use this link to download a Postman collection for the Invoice APIs. The complete documentation for the Vendor v3.1 API can be found on the SAP Concur Developer Center. Note that this collection assumes you’re using the environment from the authentication blog linked above. You will need an application with the following scope:

  • INVVEN

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Invoice – Standard
  • Invoice - Professional

 

Introduction

One of the biggest benefits of using APIs over a flat file is the near real-time data integration ability. Rather than trying to schedule periodic syncing of vendor data, the best-practice approach is to implement an event-driven integration. When a new vendor is created in your system of record, immediately push the vendor to Concur. When a change is made to an existing vendor and the Save button is clicked, immediately push that change to Concur. This not only ensures that Concur is always up-to-date with the latest changes, but also mitigates any performance issues that may occur if you’re trying to sync hundreds or thousands of vendor records at a time.

 

Create or Update a Vendor

Let’s start by creating a basic vendor record. Within the Vendor Postman collection, expand the folder called ‘Vendors’ and click on the ‘Create / Update Vendor’ request:

 The following fields are always required for every vendor:

  • AddressCode
  • VendorCode
  • Country
  • Currency

Other fields could be required depending on the configuration of your specific Concur site and the business requirements that were defined. If the VendorName field is not provided, the system will use the VendorCode as the name. Some fields such as PaymentMethodType and PaymentTerms will use system-configured default values if nothing is supplied; therefore, the best practice is to explicitly provide a value where possible. This collection includes every possible available field for completeness; you may omit unused fields from your PUT request.

The schema to update a vendor is exactly the same as it is to create a vendor. The combination of VendorCode and AddressCode is what determines a distinct vendor in Concur. Once these values are set, they cannot be changed. If either of these values needs to be updated, you’ll need to delete the original vendor and create it as a new record with the updated codes.

You may provide multiple vendor records to create or update within the request body. A 200 response code along with the number of records processed is the expected response:

The PUT request to update a vendor is handled as a full-replace; with the exception of the DefaultExpenseTypeName and DefaultEmployeeID fields, any field you don’t explicitly send a value for will get nulled, even if it previously had a value. The Vendor API does not support the PATCH operation.

 

Retrieve Vendors

Next, we’ll look at how to retrieve a vendor. Switch to the Get Vendor Information request. There are many different parameters that you can use to search / filter for vendors. These are detailed on the Params tab of the request, highlighted in yellow below. For example, we could search by vendorCode to validate that the vendor we sent above was created as expected:

Note that the response will include every available field, even if no value is set for that particular vendor record.

Your Invoice configuration may allow end-users to request a new vendor within the Concur UI. This is useful if they need to enter an invoice for a vendor that doesn’t already exist. However, the vendor will initially be created as an unapproved vendor; before any invoices assigned to that vendor can be fully approved and extracted, that vendor must be approved. The GET vendor endpoint can be used to retrieve these user-created vendors by setting the approved parameter to false:

If this vendor is ultimately approved, you can use the details in this response to add the vendor to your system of record. Normally we use the VendorCode and AddressCode when determining whether to create or update a vendor, but unapproved vendors have random values created for these (as you can see in the above screenshot). Take note of the AddressImportSyncID value instead:

After this vendor has been created in your system of record and gets a true vendor code created, send a PUT request with the details back to Concur. Unlike with a new vendor, this time we’ll need the AddressImportSyncID value populated in the PUT request (this is normally left blank). There will be an additional message in the response noting that we have updated the Unapproved vendor:

 

Add Vendor Group

Concur Invoice has a concept called Vendor Access Groups, which allows you to limit which vendors a given set of employees has access to. Click on the Add Vendor Group (Employee Access Mapping) Request. There are two parts to adding a vendor group – you must have the vendor code and address code populated in the parameters:

 And you add the specific group(s) you want to add within the body of the request:

This API requires the name of the vendor group as it’s configured within Invoice Groups:

Contact your Invoice Administrator to get a list of valid vendor access group names.

Once a group has been added to a vendor, it will be included in the VendorGroupList section of the GET Vendor response:

Note that every vendor will always be assigned to the Global group.

To remove a Vendor Group, click on the Delete Vendor Group request:

This differs slightly in that the group to be deleted is added as a parameter to the request, rather than being sent in the body of the request as when adding a group.

 

Vendor Banking Info

If you utilize ACH payments via Concur Invoice Pay, then we need to have that vendor’s banking information on file. This can be added using the Add Vendor Banking Request:

If Banking Details have been added to a vendor record, they will be included in the Get Vendor response:

 

Delete Vendor

If you no longer do business with a given vendor, then that record should be marked as deleted. That will remove it from the list of available vendors that employees can choose from when creating invoices. Select the Delete Vendor request:

The vendor and address codes must be provided in the parameters. Note that any invoices that were assigned to that vendor that were not already fully approved will need to be assigned to a different vendor before they can proceed in the approval workflow.

 

Conclusion

The Vendor APIs give you more flexibility and control over when vendors are created / updated versus the typical Overnight Job schedule / file delivered to SFTP. Using the information in this post and the accompanying Postman collection, you should be on your way to a more efficient vendor management experience.

Stay tuned for the next post in this series, where we’ll cover how to manage Purchase Requests.

 

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.

Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: BindingObject of a selected list picker item
entry.content:
@bill_froelichYes, probably this is the problem.

For debug purpose I had an older MDK Client(23+) for build and debug apps in VSCode.

I downloaded the 24.7.3 MDKClient. Now i have a new problem, after "create-client" and after "tns run android --device XXX" i can't authenticate in the app because after the Log In Button i got a "HTTP Status 400".

The BrandedSettings.json was the same of previous app, I checked all the ConnectionSettings and seems to be ok.

What else I can check ?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020
entry.content:
Crystal Reports Designer doesn't provide such functionality.

But Ken Hamady maintains a listing of 3rd-party Crystal Reports schedulers here.

entry.title: Vower member
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Melinda , and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m an eager and outgoing learner . On my spare time I enjoy researching the latest Ai trend a or Studying . I have a background in customer service and also Business

 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in all area of SAP . I am in hope of finding a way to gain experience and knowledge of SAP. I’m currently a junior in College pursuing a Bachelor degree in Cybersecurity.

 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because pursuing the SAP certification – whether it’s career growth or opportunity or getting an insight of of more technology skills .

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in networking , mentors and anything with technology !!!

 

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Melinda Lee  

College student ( Future Cyber Security Engineer)

entry.title: Re: Periodo de Contabilizacion Bloqueado Especifique dato...
entry.content: al utilizar la transaccion CO15 y tratar de registrar Período contabilización 010 2024 está bloqueado (verifique fe.contabile).

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my keen interest in the opportunity to take the SAP exam for free. Last year, I made the difficult decision to leave my career in e-commerce after more than six years to focus on my family, welcoming my second child. Since then, while balancing childcare, I have dedicated my time to studying management accounting and exploring a career as an SAP consultant.

As a woman in my thirties starting anew in this field, I am acutely aware of the challenges I face in gaining a foothold in a male-dominated industry. Obtaining the SAP certification would significantly enhance my job prospects and provide me with the skills necessary to contribute meaningfully in this space.

I truly appreciate any consideration for this opportunity, as it could be a pivotal step in my career transition.

Thank you for your time and support.

Harumi

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Unique Constraint Violated Error [301]
entry.content: maybe it get called twice.

entry.title: Validation of supplier in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

I want to get the warning message for supplier if more than one bank account is added in bank accounts entity. But I'm getting warning message after entering Bank country/Region, Bank key of second bank account.

Please find below code for the same:

DATA : lv_row_index     TYPE i VALUE 0.


    cl_usmd_app_context=>get_context( )->get_attributes(
                  IMPORTING
                    ev_crequest_id = DATA(lv_cr_num6)                     "CR Number
                    ev_crequest_type  = DATA(lv_cr_type6)                 " Type of Change Request
                ).
    IF lv_cr_type6 = 'ZSUPP1' OR lv_cr_type6 = 'ZSUPP2'.

      IF id_entitytype = 'BP_BKDTL'.
        LOOP AT it_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data>).
          IF <fs_data> IS ASSIGNED.
            lv_row_index = lv_row_index + 1.
          ENDIF.
          IF lv_row_index > 1.
            message-msgid = 'ZMDG'.
            message-msgty = 'W'.
            message-msgno = '008'.
            APPEND message TO et_message.
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.

      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

Anyone please suggest which code changes should i need to do for getting warning message without entering Bank country/region, bank key for second bank details id.

entry.title: Best Practices for Data Migration in a Carve-Out from S/4HANA On-Prem to S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I’m currently working with a customer who is performing a carve-out, moving from their legacy S/4HANA On-Premise system to S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. As part of this project, they are facing challenges in identifying the relevant data tables for migration and pinpointing where exactly the necessary data resides in their existing system. I’d like to reach out to this community for any best practices or lessons learned from similar carve-out scenarios.

  • The new system will be based on S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, so we are focusing on a clean and efficient transition.
  • The legacy system is S/4HANA On-Premise, with years of data accumulated. The customer is unsure about the best approach to identify and extract only the necessary data for migration. 

Challenges:


  1. 1. Identifying relevant tables: The customer is unsure about which tables to migrate and how to locate the key tables in their On-Premise system.
    2. Data relevance: There’s some confusion around how to extract **only the relevant data**, without transferring unnecessary historical data.
    3. Table mapping for migration tools: Aligning On-Prem tables with SAP Migration Cockpit templates and the standard migration objects available for the Cloud edition has also proven to be challenging.

Key Questions:

  • What best practices exist for identifying and mapping the critical tables in S/4HANA On-Prem for a carve-out scenario?
  • Are there any efficient methodologies or tools that can help pinpoint relevant data and ensure it aligns with the S/4HANA Cloud public edition data structure?
  • How do you ensure the customer extracts only the required data, avoiding clutter or excessive historical records? 

I’d appreciate any advice, strategies, or best practices that you have found effective in your own experience—particularly in scenarios where S/4HANA On-Prem is being transitioned to the public Cloud edition. We want to ensure that our data migration is as clean and efficient as possible.

Thanks in advance for your input!

Best regards,
Yannick

entry.title: Re: Advice on Reducing DHCDC_RTSTORE Table Size in S/4HANA
entry.content:
@n_gaertner :  I have edited my response. However, in my earlier response, I had mentioned that OP has to evaluate his needs. It's up to the OP to do due diligence and decide. I understand that some options can be risky (not dangerous). When an initialization load is executed, if the load is completed successfully, the data from table DHCDC_RTSTORE gets deleted automatically. But if the load fails, the data gets stuck in DHCDC_RTSTORE and need manual intervention.  https://help.sap.com/doc/d6a3fa1bdb894bf39296d3306e73db45/1.0.04/en-US/SLT%20Performance%20Guide_Nov_2022.pdf .

Thanks for your understanding and suggestion.

entry.title: difficulties to tracking the alignment between the requirements and the material provided by PPC

Please assist us by generating this report so that we can ensure accuracy and consistency between the requirements and material allocations.

entry.title: Re: USMM2 measurment 1160 fails with error You are not authorized to execute this program.

entry.content:
I'm hoping someone can help me with an issue I'm having with the Manage buffer level app xnx.

Cl𝚒ck Here To link Hot xxx indian videos 

I'd be really grateful if you could help me work out what's causing this. I'd be really grateful if you could suggest something. Proposal for Product XXXX Plant XXXX MRP Area XXXX can't be adopted in Manage buffer level app xxx.jpg I'd also love to share another solution for an issue that I think you'll find really helpful! I hope you're having a lovely day! I just wanted to quickly share how I managed to solve another little problem I was having. It was regarding the job template SAP_PPDD_PRODBUFF_MASSACTION_J. I found that the SIMG – ABAP Platform – Application Server – System Administration – Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalog Entries option was exactly what I needed to resolve it. I'm so pleased I found that!

#Enterprise Resource Planning

entry.title: Re: How and where do you start a consulting career in SAP?
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I’m excited about this opportunity to enhance my digital skills. With over 2 years of experience and some part-time freelancing, We are currently working in core SAP modules, as well as planned to implement more Module as per company requirement, which I’m especially interested in.

I’ve been expanding my knowledge through YouTube and online resources. Although I’m eager to pursue an group reporting certification, financial constraints make it challenging right now, and job opportunities in my country are limited to experienced professionals.

I welcome any feedback and look forward to further developing my expertise in SAP.

Hi @David_Chaviano 

I’m excited about this opportunity to enhance my digital skills. With over 2 years of experience and some part-time freelancing, We are currently working in core SAP modules, as well as planned to implement more Module as per company requirement, which I’m especially interested in.

I’ve been expanding my knowledge through YouTube and online resources. Although I’m eager to pursue an group reporting certification, financial constraints make it challenging right now, and job opportunities in my country are limited to experienced professionals.

I welcome any feedback and look forward to further developing my expertise in SAP.

I will be more than appreciated if I would get chance to participate related SAP MM trainings and build a career with it. 

Mina.

entry.title: Re: Multiple entry option in Input parameters of Graphica...
entry.content: Hi Joe, yes, correct. I do a text assoc to the field and add a filter variable to the text assoc, not the field. Performance, well, always persist. You do not need to switch the table to in-memory, disk is sufficient, that way you will not consume much resources. Would this work in your case? Thanks, Martin

entry.title: Real-time OpenAI Response Streaming
entry.content:
The Realtime API enables you to build low-latency, multi-modal conversational experiences. It currently supports text and audio as both input and output, as well as function calling.

Quickstart

The Realtime API is a WebSocket interface that is designed to run on the server. To help you get started quickly, we've created a console demo application that shows some of the features of the API.

Overview

The Realtime API is a stateful, event-based API that communicates over a WebSocket. The WebSocket connection requires the following parameters:

  • URL: wss://api.openai.com/v1/realtime
  • Query Parameters: ?model=gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-10-01
  • Headers:
    • Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
    • OpenAI-Beta: realtime=v1

      Below is a simple example using Python - FastAPi

      📦openAI-realtime
 ┣ 📂app
          ┗  📂routes
          ┗  📜 health_check.py
          ┗  📜 realtime.py
         ┗  📜 config.py
         ┗  📜 main.py
      ┣ 📂client 
         ┗  📜 client.html

Create a .env file in the root directory with the following content. Use the appropriate URL depending on whether you are using OpenAI or Azure OpenAI:

OPENAI_API_KEY="your-openai-api-key"
OPENAI_REALTIME_URL="wss://api.openai.com/v1/realtime?model=gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-10-01"
USE_AZURE_OPENAI=False
 

For Azure OpenAI, replace the URL as follows and set USE_AZURE_OPENAI to True:

OPENAI_API_KEY="your-azure-openai-api-key"
OPENAI_REALTIME_URL="wss://<sub-domain>.openai.azure.com/openai/realtime?api-version=2024-10-01-preview&deployment=gpt-4o-realtime-preview"
USE_AZURE_OPENAI=True
 
 

app/routes/realtime.py

 

 

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import websockets
import traceback

from fastapi import WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect, APIRouter
from app.config import VENDOR_WS_URL, API_KEY, USE_AZURE_OPENAI

realtime_router = APIRouter()

# Set up logging
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.DEBUG, format="%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
)

# Determine appropriate headers
extra_headers = (
    {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {API_KEY}",
        "openai-beta": "realtime=v1",
    }
    if USE_AZURE_OPENAI
    else {"api-key": API_KEY}
)


async def relay_messages(client_ws: WebSocket, vendor_ws):
    """Relay messages between client and vendor WebSockets."""

    async def client_to_vendor():
        try:
            while True:
                data = await client_ws.receive_json()
                if data and json_validator(data):
                    await vendor_ws.send(json.dumps(data))
                else:
                    warning_msg = "Invalid data: payload should be JSON."
                    logging.warning(warning_msg)
                    await send_text_safe(client_ws, warning_msg)
        except WebSocketDisconnect:
            logging.info("Client WebSocket disconnected.")
        except Exception as e:
            print(traceback.format_exc())

            logging.error(f"Error in client_to_vendor: {e}")

    async def vendor_to_client():
        try:
            while True:
                data = await vendor_ws.recv()
                await client_ws.send_text(data)
        except websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosed as e:
            logging.info(f"Vendor WebSocket disconnected: {e}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(traceback.format_exc())

            logging.error(f"Error in vendor_to_client: {e}")

    tasks = [
        asyncio.create_task(client_to_vendor()),
        asyncio.create_task(vendor_to_client()),
    ]
    done, pending = await asyncio.wait(tasks, return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED)
    for task in pending:
        task.cancel()
        await asyncio.gather(task, return_exceptions=True)


@realtime_router.websocket("/realtime")
async def websocket_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket):
    """Handle WebSocket connections from clients."""
    client_ip = websocket.client.host
    logging.info(f"Client connected: {client_ip}")
    await websocket.accept()

    try:
        async with websockets.connect(
            VENDOR_WS_URL, extra_headers=extra_headers
        ) as vendor_ws:
            logging.info("Connected to vendor WebSocket.")
            await relay_messages(websocket, vendor_ws)
    except websockets.exceptions.InvalidHandshake as e:
        error_msg = f"Vendor WebSocket handshake failed: {e}"
        logging.error(error_msg)
        await send_text_safe(websocket, error_msg)
    except WebSocketDisconnect:
        logging.info(f"Client disconnected: {client_ip}")
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
        await send_text_safe(websocket, f"Unexpected error: {e}")


async def send_text_safe(ws: WebSocket, message: str):
    """Safely send messages to the client WebSocket."""
    try:
        await ws.send_text(message)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Error sending message to client: {e}")


def json_validator(data) -> bool:
    """Validate if the input data is JSON."""
    try:
        print(f"data: {data}")
        # Check if data is already a dict, which is valid JSON in Python
        if isinstance(data, dict):
            return True
        # Check if the input is a non-empty string
        if isinstance(data, str) and data.strip() == "":
            return False
        # If data is a string, try to load it as JSON
        json.loads(data)
        return True
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
        return False

 

 

app/routes/health_check.py

 

 

from fastapi import APIRouter
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

health_check_router = APIRouter()


@health_check_router.get("/")
async def health_check():
    return JSONResponse(content={"status": "Iam Alive!"}, status_code=200)

 

 

app/config.py

 

 

import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load environment variables from .env file
load_dotenv()

# Configuration variables
VENDOR_WS_URL = os.getenv("OPENAI_REALTIME_URL")
API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
USE_AZURE_OPENAI = bool(os.getenv("USE_AZURE_OPENAI"))

 

 

app/main.py

 

 

from fastapi import FastAPI
from app.routes.health_check import health_check_router
from app.routes.realtime import realtime_router

app = FastAPI()

# Registering routers
app.include_router(health_check_router)
app.include_router(realtime_router)

 

 

client/client.html

 

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>WebSocket Client</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>WebSocket Client</h1>
    <button id="connectButton">Connect to WebSocket</button>
    <br><br>
    <textarea id="messageInput" placeholder="Enter your message here..." rows="4" cols="50"></textarea>
    <br>
    <button id="sendButton" disabled>Send Message</button>
    <h2>Messages:</h2>
    <pre id="messages"></pre>

    <script>
        let websocket;
        const connectButton = document.getElementById('connectButton');
        const sendButton = document.getElementById('sendButton');
        const messageInput = document.getElementById('messageInput');
        const messagesDisplay = document.getElementById('messages');

        connectButton.addEventListener('click', () => {
            websocket = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:8000/realtime');

            websocket.onopen = () => {
                messagesDisplay.textContent += 'Connected to WebSocket server.\n';
                sendButton.disabled = false;
            };

            websocket.onmessage = (event) => {
                messagesDisplay.textContent += 'Received: ' + event.data + '\n';
            };

            websocket.onclose = () => {
                messagesDisplay.textContent += 'WebSocket connection closed.\n';
                sendButton.disabled = true;
            };

            websocket.onerror = (error) => {
                messagesDisplay.textContent += 'WebSocket error: ' + error.message + '\n';
            };
        });

        sendButton.addEventListener('click', () => {
            const message = messageInput.value.trim();
            if (message !== '') {
                websocket.send(message);
                messagesDisplay.textContent += 'Sent: ' + message + '\n';
                messageInput.value = '';
            } else {
                alert('Please enter a message to send.');
            }
        });
    </script>
</body>
</html>

 

 

requirements.txt

fastapi==0.115.0
uvicorn==0.31.0
websockets==13.1
python-dotenv==1.0.1

Install dependencies:

 

 

pip install -r requirements.txt<div class=""><h2>Usage<a class="" href="https://github.com/Geo-Joy/openai-realtime-fastapi/blob/main/README.md#usage" target="_blank"><p>To start the application, use Uvicorn to run the FastAPI server:<li-code lang="abap">uvicorn main:app --reload

 

 

 

The application should now be running on http://localhost:8000.

 

Reference Links

  • Realtime API Documentation
  • Azure Realtime Audio SDK Samples

entry.title: Career Progression Advice
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am certified in S4 HANA FI 1709 and certified in S4 HANA CFIN. I have close to 5 yrs of experience in CFIN - Run and Maintenance , Treasury experience in IHC, BAM of 1.5 years , with overall 9 years of experience. Please guide me as to how to advance my career further as at this point of time I feel stuck. Any guidance would be highly appreciated.
